intro: Quick onboarding so Arkive knows how to behave. Eight questions. You can change any answer later by saying 'reconfigure ARK'.

questions.title: How strictly should rules in your rules/ arkive be enforced?
questions.description: When a rule's action is `warn` or `require_override`, do I lean toward letting things through or holding the line?

options.label: Lenient
options.description: Downgrade rule actions: `warn` becomes silent, `require_override` becomes `warn`, `block` stays `block`.

options.label: Normal
options.description: Apply rule actions exactly as defined in each rule entry.

options.label: Strict
options.description: Upgrade rule actions: `warn` becomes `require_override`, `require_override` becomes `block` (only for safety/sizing categories).

questions.title: How much should I say in responses?
questions.description: Affects all non-confirmation output. Required confirmations and rule warnings are never suppressed.

options.label: Terse
options.description: Minimum viable response. Bullet points, no preamble.

options.label: Normal
options.description: Full explanations when useful, brief when obvious.

options.label: Thorough
options.description: Detailed reasoning, alternatives considered, edge cases noted.

questions.title: What's your familiarity with on-chain trading?
questions.description: Affects how much I unpack jargon (slippage, gas, FoT, MEV, bridge, etc).

options.label: New to this
options.description: Define jargon the first time it appears in a session. Spell things out.

options.label: Comfortable
options.description: Use trader vocabulary freely. Define only the obscure stuff.

options.label: Power user
options.description: Full jargon, no hand-holding. Skip 101 explanations entirely.

questions.title: How willing should I be to push back on your requests?
questions.description: Affects whether I voice concerns about the trade you asked for, or just queue it.

options.label: Compliant
options.description: Execute what's asked. Surface only blocks and required confirmations.

options.label: Balanced
options.description: Speak up about non-trivial risks (low liquidity, high impact, fresh token) before executing.

options.label: Challenging
options.description: Question rationale on borderline calls. Suggest alternatives. Reference patterns/ when relevant.

questions.title: When should I ask before acting?
questions.description: Independent of rules — this is about my conversational habit, not enforcement.

options.label: Always
options.description: Confirm before every action that touches state — including reads of preferences, writes to rules, swaps.

options.label: Writes + financial
options.description: Confirm before any write to arkives or any swap. Reads are silent.

options.label: Financial only
options.description: Confirm only before queueing a swap. Arkive writes are silent.

options.label: Never
options.description: Just do it. Block-level rules still gate; confirmation prompts disappear.

questions.title: What chain do you want as default?
questions.description: Used when you say 'buy PEPE' without specifying a chain. You can override per request anytime.

options.label: Ethereum
options.description: L1, deepest liquidity, higher gas.

options.label: Base
options.description: L2, cheap gas (~$0.005/swap), Coinbase-backed.

options.label: Ask each time
options.description: I'll prompt for chain if you don't specify.

questions.title: What's a 'dust' position to you?
questions.description: Holdings worth less than this are filtered out of portfolio summaries and PnL views by default. Includes scam airdrops and rounding scraps. Still queryable via includeDust=true.

options.description: Strict — only ignore truly trivial scraps.
Recommended. Filters scam airdrops and pennies.
Aggressive. Hides anything not worth selling for gas.

options.label: Show all
options.description: No filter. Dust + scam tokens included in everything.
